name: Focaccia Rustica (Country Focaccia with Red Pepper Toppin
recipeInstructions:
Topping:
3 red or yellow bell peppers, sliced into thin strips
3 medium red onions, thinly sliced
1/4 cup olive oil
4 medium ripe tomatoes, seeded and squeezed dry
1 basil leaf
1 tsp salt
1. Warm the garlic cloves in the olive oil over low heat until the garlic begins to brown. Discard the garlic and let the oil cool.
2. Meanwhile, whisk the yeast into the water and let proof for 10 minutes. Add the cooled olive oil. Stir in the salt, flour, and herbs and mix well.
3. Knead by hand for 6 to 8 minutes. Place dough into a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and let rise until doubled.
4. Divide the dough in half and set each piece on an oiled baking sheet (10-1/2" X 15-1/2"). Cover and let it relax for 15 minutes. Dimple and stretch the dough some more.
5. Let rise until puffy, about 50 minutes.
6. While it is rising, roast, peel, and slice the peppers into thin strips. Saute the onions in the olive oil over very low heat for 20 to 25 minutes until they are soft and limp.
7. Preheat the oven to 400°F. Lightly dip your fingers into the remaining olive oil and dimple the tops of the dough.
8. Divide the vegetables evenly among half and distribute them over the focaccia. Sprinkle with basil and salt.
9. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes until the dough is crispy on the edges. Cool briefly, then remove from the sheets and let cool on racks. Serve at room temperature.
